notes: Token reporting is the LOAD-BEARING observable: the opencode parser pulls step-finish.tokens.input/output and the nested cache.read/cache.write into UsageBreakdown{Input,Output,CacheRead,CacheCreation5m}, and SessionMetrics folds each PerTurnContribution into a monotonic accumulator (CumInputTokens/CumOutputTokens/CumCacheReadTokens). This evidence is NOT expressible in the phase DSL (no TotalTokens / cache-bucket metric form), so it lives in events.jsonl + the replay .golden summary (cum_input_tokens > 0, cum_output_tokens > 0). The recipe drives THREE chained --session `opencode run` turns so the monotonic-accumulation invariant is exercised across two boundaries (T1→T2, T2→T3) in the captured token data. The phase DSL asserts only the per-turn STATE arcs the accounting rides on. DE-PINNED the third separable state arc (former t3_start/t3_end): opencode spawns a FRESH short-lived process per `opencode run`, and the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore watcher observes the SQLite store via WAL-write-triggered scans with a 500ms minScanGap debounce (not a per-turn stream), so two back-to-back same-ses_ runs land their WAL writes inside one scan window and COALESCE their state arcs into one observed working→ready — turn 3's working span collapses to zero width (golden: turn-3 ready→working + working→ready share virtual_time with cause=debounce_coalesce). That coalescing is a transport property of poll-based observation of per-run processes, NOT a token-accounting defect and NOT recipe-fixable (the inter-turn sleeps are already ≥5s; tuning them to separate two same-ses_ processes would be tuning to the recording, not the spec). The two separable arcs (t1/t2) prove ready→working→ready cycling and the monotonic-accumulation boundary; asserting a third over-specifies the vehicle through a transport that cannot separate rapid same-session turns. cost_tracking=false is HONEST — the default local LM Studio model (gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored) reports cost=0, so EstimatedCostUSD reads $0; the spec asserts token COUNTS/deltas (real), never a nonzero dollar cost. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; t1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity that t1_end/t2_start/t2_end chain off via same_session_as. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant covers tool_calls_max:0 — the no-tool turns never gate on a permission ask. Token & cost extraction for a single turn is independently covered by basic-turn; this scenario stresses per-turn accumulation across multiple turns.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
PID bound to the ses_… session
Turn 1 user prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
Turn 1 completes back to ready on the same ses_… session; first PerTurnContribution flushed, TotalTokens > 0
Turn 2 user prompt → working on the same ses_… session
Turn 2 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic accumulation across turns)
